etymology.description: 水(水) (shuǐ, "water") + 喿(喿) (zào, used phonetically) = interaction with water -> bathe
etymology.interpretation: The character 澡(zǎo) combines the semantic element 水(水) (shuǐ, "water") with the phonetic element 喿(喿) (zào) to convey the idea of washing or bathing with water.
etymology.simplification: In simplified Chinese, 澡 remains the same, as no simplified variant exists.

examples.chinese: 每天晚上他都会洗澡(每天晚上他都會洗澡)。
examples.pinyin: Měitiān wǎnshàng tā dōu huì xǐzǎo.
examples.english: He takes a bath every night.

examples.chinese: 孩子们喜欢在阳光下玩水后去洗澡(孩子們喜歡在陽光下玩水後去洗澡)。
examples.pinyin: Háizimen xǐhuān zài yángguāng xià wán shuǐ hòu qù xǐzǎo.
examples.english: The children like to bathe after playing in the sun.

examples.chinese: 早上的淋浴让他感到精神焕发(早上的淋浴讓他感到精神煥發)。
examples.pinyin: Zǎoshang de línyù ràng tā gǎndào jīngshén huànfā.
examples.english: The morning shower makes him feel refreshed.
